components.notes: Grounded from the canonical button matrix and link-specific documentation page. This is the current source of truth for button size, style, type, state, and icon-only naming.

figmaNodes.notes: Top-level button page that contains the main matrix and link treatment.
Canonical variant matrix for contained, outlined, and text buttons, including icon-only variants.
Link-style treatment used when `PButton` is rendered with link-like emphasis.
Usage guidance and anatomy for main buttons.
Usage guidance for link-style button behavior.

components.notes: Grounded from the icons page and its documentation. This page is the current source for available icon names and usage constraints.

figmaNodes.notes: Top-level icon page.
Canonical icon catalog, including names such as `Search`, `Menu`, `Chevron--down`, and Partner-specific icons.
Usage guidance states icons are not standalone interactive elements and should remain within fixed sizing constraints.

components.notes: Grounded from the logo page and logo matrix for brand variants, favicon variants, and brand-specific lockups.

figmaNodes.notes: Top-level logo page.
Canonical logo matrix covering Partner, SiteLynx, BlueLynx, Plink-Live, and favicon variants.

components.notes: Grounded from the typography table. This is the current source of truth for typography variant names that map to `PTypography`.

figmaNodes.notes: Canonical typography table confirming names such as `h1`, `headline 1`, `body 1`, `inputLabel`, and `tooltip`.

components.notes: Grounded from the layout grid page. This is the current source of truth for breakpoint, gutter, margin, and alignment rules that back the layout grid primitives in the repo.

figmaNodes.notes: Top-level layout grid page.
Canonical breakpoint table for `xl`, `lg`, `md`, and `sm`, plus example body container layouts.
Documentation describing how body content aligns below the main navigation bar and responds across breakpoints.

components.notes: Mapped to the same grid system source as `PLayoutGrid`. The Figma page defines the column structure and container behavior that individual grid items should honor.

figmaNodes.notes: Canonical column and breakpoint system used by grid items.

components.notes: The Figma main navigation bar exists, but there is no public Partner component in this repo yet. Keep this mapping so the implementation can be attached once the component is built.

figmaNodes.notes: Top-level main navigation page.
Canonical main navigation bar with left and center alignment variants.
Reference frame for nav items, nav groups, icon groups, and page labels.
Documentation describing anatomy, variants, responsiveness, and token usage. Attach this to the component once implementation begins.

components.notes: The side navigation panel exists in Figma, but there is no public Partner component in this repo yet. Keep this mapping for future implementation sync.

figmaNodes.notes: Top-level side nav page.
Default side nav item variants across extra large, large, medium, and small sizes.
Secondary side nav item treatment for nested or lower-emphasis navigation.

components.notes: Breadcrumb exists in Figma but there is no public breadcrumb component in this repo yet. Preserve the grounded nodes so implementation can pick up the correct overflow behavior later.

figmaNodes.notes: Top-level breadcrumb page.
Canonical breadcrumb states including current page treatment and overflow behavior.

components.notes: Grounded from the pagination page and documentation. This is the current source of truth for pagination items, small variants, and truncation behavior.

figmaNodes.notes: Top-level pagination page.
Canonical pagination frame covering pagination nav, item states, and truncation examples.
Documentation covering truncation, placement, and small-mode usage.

components.notes: Grounded from the checkbox page and documentation. This is the current source of truth for checkbox sizes, disabled states, and checked vs unchecked treatment.

figmaNodes.notes: Top-level checkbox page.
Canonical checkbox field variants across large, medium, and small sizes.
Reference item states including indeterminate and hover behavior.
Documentation covering group usage, indeterminate behavior, and validation guidance.

components.notes: Mapped to the same checkbox page because the canonical group patterns live there and document horizontal, vertical, and error-state behavior.

figmaNodes.notes: Contains `Checkbox Group` variants and error-state examples.

components.notes: Grounded from the select page. This is the current source of truth for single-select field states, multi-select treatment, and size variants.

figmaNodes.notes: Top-level select page.
Canonical single-select dropdown states plus multi-select examples with chips and overflow counts.

components.notes: Grounded from the menu system plus the user-provided select-menu reference frame. This mapping covers trigger-driven selection overlays with search, checkbox items, Select All, and footer actions like Clear and Apply.

figmaNodes.notes: Top-level menu page containing the select-menu patterns.
Reference frame for `PSelectMenu` variants, including searchable multi-select states, Select All behavior, secondary text options, and Clear/Apply footer actions.

components.notes: A Page Header page exists in Figma, but the supplied node currently resolves only to the top-level canvas. Keep this placeholder and refine it later with a deeper frame URL once the design is further along or implementation begins.

figmaNodes.notes: Top-level page header canvas. Needs a deeper frame mapping once the source page is expanded.

components.notes: The tab system exists in Figma, but there is no public Partner tab component in this repo yet. Preserve this mapping so a future implementation can inherit the correct orientation, active-state, and count behavior.

figmaNodes.notes: Top-level tab page.
Canonical tab-item states for horizontal and vertical tabs, plus small and default sizes.
Reference for number count styling and tab-item subparts.
Despite the frame name, this is the tab documentation page. It documents tab anatomy, orientation, counts, and active-state behavior.

components.notes: Date input exists in Figma, but there is no public Partner date input component in this repo yet. Preserve this mapping for a future implementation.

figmaNodes.notes: Top-level date input and picker page.
Canonical date input states for large, medium, and small sizes with and without values.
Despite the frame name, this is the date input documentation page. It documents format, validation, and input rules.

components.notes: Calendar picker exists in Figma, but there is no public Partner date picker component in this repo yet. Preserve the grounded nodes for future implementation.

figmaNodes.notes: Canonical calendar picker with year-select variants.

components.notes: Grounded from the dialog page and the user-provided canonical dialog frame. The canonical node is shared with the confirmation modal patterns currently represented in Figma.

components.notes: Grounded from the menu page plus user-provided canonical and documentation nodes. In Figma, `Menu` is a shared primitive page that also contains select-menu patterns. Map to `PMenu` only for generic action menus and flexible dropdown content; use `PSelectMenu` for searchable or selection-oriented overlays with checkbox items, Select All, or Clear/Apply footer actions.

figmaNodes.notes: Top-level menu page.

components.notes: Grounded from the menu page plus user-provided menu-item canonical and documentation nodes. Do not use this mapping for full menu example frames that represent selectable overlay patterns; those should map to `PSelectMenu`.
